transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
The context is that Jason said that what they're expecting is movement in the hundreds of thousands in terms of like actual traffic coming to the website.
The current forecast doesn't really say that.
So we sort of need to expand our content backlog to actually show what growth can realistically look like.
So one factor of that was just that we had about 180 topics so far.
And we would get through them in about a couple of months.
And the forecast is sort of accurate for that.
But we need to dramatically expand the content backlog to like about at least six months of data just so we can build out a better forecast that they can actually get excited about.
So in order to do that, I just wanted to take a step back and sort of let you talk me through how you sort of arrived at the current strategy.
Just like general is fine.
But once you've done that, I do want to talk about the exact process you followed to get the keywords that you arrived at.
So what was the workflow for the keyword research?
And maybe I can help there to sort of get that up as soon as running.
Yeah.
So let me share.
I don't know if you looked at the content OS, but we basically mapped out these different topic clusters, some of which are specifically focused on programmers.
And this is where I haven't done as much keyword research because this will take more time to validate all of these ideas.
And I think that will add a lot more articles to our content roadmap because there are so many different topics we can cover here.
Sorry to interrupt you.
How did we arrive upon these topic clusters?
Like what was the process from the onboarding call to us coming up with these topic clusters?
Yeah.
So they shared that.
Their priority is getting specifically coders as data annotation workers.
So we did research based on that specific ICP to find out what kind of topics would be useful for them.
So, of course, there's things that are more specific to data, like AI training education.
Also, what's helpful to more experienced coders would be career advice, interview tips, so they can move up to higher roles.
And then also, like learning resources, basically.
To attract those high-quality coders.
what did the process look like to exactly arrive upon these topic clusters?
So what I'm trying to sort of understand is that, like, the spectrum of possibility when we start with any client is essentially infinite, right?
So what is our process for narrowing that down to exactly what the client wants?
Like, for example, we have about six topic clusters here.
But if I were talking to another strategist, they might come up with a different plan.
So I'm just trying to understand what your process was and what other people do at the company, because I feel like there is a bit of variability there.
And I'm just trying to get as much context as possible for how we create it.
Yeah, so I mean, I think part of it was just brainstorming together.
I'm also looking back into what I was doing in Quad.

We were just brainstorming based on, not in person, but as people, brainstorming these different topics and then taking that information and what we know about the target audience and putting that into Cloud and asking it to help me map out multiple options of topic clusters.
It came up with a lot of options, but they're not all worth pursuing.
I think the kind of question, yes, like someone could come up with like 10 topic clusters, but is it really like in terms of prioritizing and seeing where we could get the most wins?
It's probably not all 10.
It's going to be a smaller amount.
Okay.
So during this process, do you guys look at competitors and what they're doing?
Like, does that factor into the selection process?
Yeah.

Although I think the tricky or like the kind of conflicting thing with data annotation in particular is they don't have a ton of competitors and their competitor content isn't nearly as extensive as like, let's say if you're in a different industry or with a different client with their competitors have like, an existing content library.

I think the main competitors for data annotation are things like ScaleAI, Appen, and they're like, the way that they cover content is not extensive enough for us to kind of like get ideas from them and do a more typical content gap analysis there.
Okay.
Makes sense.
So like, aside from like direct competitors, do we look at indirect competitors?
So for example, if you want to own the cluster for career advice for experienced developers, right?
There might not be a lot of direct competitors, but there definitely are people who would be competing with us in the search results.
they might not do the exact same thing we do, but there might be like topical competitors.
So do we factor that forward?
Yeah, that's a good point.
For interview tips and career advice, a lot of the comparing to competitors is things like Indeed, Glassdoor, those kind of more career-focused sites.
And so a lot of our ideas did come from there.
Okay, that makes sense.
So once you have these topic clusters mapped out, what does your keyword research process look like right now?
So I basically use Claude to generate a list of suggested keywords, like different, you know, templates or keyword strings that we can use, for instance, like staff versus senior engineer.
It's just an example.
And then going into SEMrush and using that and plugging in, actually, maybe I'll show you, I don't know if it will be faster.
Let show you.
So, for instance, going into the...let me show my screen.
Okay.
So, going here, let's just say engineer versus doing all keywords, but then including specific ones.
like, programming, senior, staff, principal.
This might not be the best example, but this is just to that.
Basically, if Quad gives me a bunch of ideas, oops, thank you.

If Quad gives me a bunch of ideas, they might not actually have any search volume, but this gives us an idea of what are the actual keywords, what their real search volume is, and then from there, that's where I'm going in and getting, you know, the intent volume and keyword difficulty that you will see in the content OS here.
So, correct me if I'm wrong.
So, basically, if we have a top of the cluster, we go to Quad.
We ask it to generate different ideas, and then we validate them with some rush.
Is that the kind of problem?
Yeah.
And I don't know that that's necessarily the best, most efficient process.
I think it kind of, if we were doing like a smaller volume, it would be fine.
But because we're trying to get hundreds of ideas, I don't think this is the best way.
But this is just, I haven't figured out a way to automate this process without essentially having like hallucinated, hallucinated keywords.
That makes sense.
Yeah, I think like the way I run keyword research is radically different from how this is.
So I'm going to mull over this a little bit and I'm probably going to record a loom on how I do it.
like generally this approach would be like a bottoms up approach where we have like a ton of clusters and then we're trying to figure out what keywords belong in those clusters.
The way I like to do it, it takes a lot more time.
So I understand that it might not be be the best fit for every scenario, but the way I do it, I just like try to get the universe of keywords that a company needs to own.
And just like it's top down.
So we have a lot of choice in terms of which lanes do we want to go in.
So that yields like a lot more keywords for our content backlog.
And I do try to use like stuff like Google's autocomplete.
People also ask questions and all the other stuff.
And I try to figure out what are the emergent themes within the current search landscape, rather than trying to do a bottom up analysis.
So let me play with this a little bit today.
If I have any other questions, I'm probably going to hit you up.
And maybe we can just like run the new process.
And I think if we do that, we should be able to come up with like 600 keywords pretty easily, I think, because I feel like the clusters we have right now, they're pretty good.
One other thing I just wanted to mention was that like, one reason the forecast wasn't super exciting at the moment was because we didn't have the salary programmatic SEO play in there.
So I feel like if we can include that into the equation.
I feel like that's going to help a lot.
So we don't need to go like super deep into it, like come up with every single permutation of keywords that we would target.
But even like the top 100 ones in terms of volume, if you add them, I feel like that's going to change the forecast quite a lot.
So I don't know if you would have the capacity to do that this week, but if you do, that would be great, but happy to help you there as needed to.
Okay.
I mean, you mean expanding from what we currently have?
Yeah, because right now we only have 15 in the programmatic salary tools cluster.
Yeah.
What I would probably do is look at Indeed or levels.fi or something similar and just try to maybe run a screaming frog analysis or just look at it in ahrefs or semcrash and see how many.
have screaming frog?
Yeah, we do.
We do.
We have it in the email account.
Right.
Yeah.
So just try to look at how many URLs exist within their particular sub directory for salary pages and that should give us like if you do.
Analysis for a couple of competitors, that should give us like a scale of how many potential URLs we can create.
And then we can just look at the top keywords for those pages and look at the search volumes.

And if you just look at the top 100 of them, that should give us like a pretty good estimate of like what kind of traffic can we drive if we publish 100 pages for the salary play within the next few months or so.
Yeah.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
I really appreciate your help with this because I know the way that I'm doing it is not the most efficient way.
Yeah.
No worries at all.
I'm highly technical when it comes to this stuff.
So like I'm more than happy to provide as much context as possible.
I definitely operate on the right side of the brain.
So.
Okay, cool.
I would just add, because you came in a little bit after Jason talked about this, Talal, but we're basically need to do the same thing with Augment.
So a little bit, yeah, customized.
But if you want to build context around that too, in the meantime, because he's also looking for the same kind of 10x growth.
So, yeah.
Okay.
Well, can keep in contact about that, but probably.
We'll also need to document this new strategy, at least like our pod's a great one for you to experiment on and then start repackaging it for each of our clients.
So data annotation is a higher priority than augment right now, right?
Always, yeah, always.
And then augment's going to be like a close second for this pod, yeah.
But it's made up for the entire company.
That's the first priority, yeah.
Okay, that sounds good.
Cool.
Let me follow up with you by tomorrow, I think.
Let me just like give it a try and see what I come up with.
And then we can just get on a call and I can talk through my process and obviously record a video too.
So you can use that going forward.
Yes, that would be awesome.
Thank you so much, Sean.
Awesome, of course.
Thank you.
Bye, everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on expanding the content backlog to meet client traffic goals.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=3.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Problem:** The current 180-topic backlog is too small for the client's goal of hundreds of thousands of monthly visitors, which requires a 6-month content plan to forecast.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=3.0)
  - [**Solution:** Replace the inefficient bottom-up keyword process (Claude → SEMrush) with a top-down approach to generate a 600+ topic backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
  - [**Immediate Action:** Add the top 100 high-volume keywords from the programmatic salary SEO play to the forecast this week to show a significant traffic boost.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
  - [**Future Plan:** Document the new process for the Data Annotation client, then adapt it for the Augment client, who has similar 10x growth goals.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)

## Topics

### The Problem: Forecasting Gap

  - [The current forecast is based on a small, 180-topic backlog that will be exhausted in \~2 months.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=16.0)
  - [This is insufficient for the client's goal of hundreds of thousands of monthly visitors.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=3.0)
  - [**Requirement:** A 6-month content plan is needed to create a more compelling forecast.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=34.0)

### Current Process: Inefficient & Limited

  - [**Topic Clusters:** Defined based on the ICP (coders) and brainstorming, with AI (Claude) used to map cluster options.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=107.0)
  - [**Keyword Research:** A bottom-up workflow:](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
    1.  [**Generate:** Use Claude to brainstorm keyword ideas for a cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
    2.  [**Validate:** Manually plug ideas into SEMrush to check for search volume and difficulty.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
  - [**Limitation:** This process is too slow for generating hundreds of ideas and risks AI "hallucinations" (keywords with no search volume).](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)

### Proposed Solution: Top-Down Keyword Research

  - [A top-down approach to efficiently generate a large-scale backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
  - [**Method:**](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
    1.  [Map the entire search landscape the client needs to own.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
    2.  [Use tools like Google Autocomplete and "People Also Ask" to find emergent themes.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
    3.  [This yields a much larger keyword universe, enabling a 600+ topic backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)

### Immediate Action: Programmatic Salary SEO

  - [Adding this play to the forecast will significantly boost projected traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
  - [**Goal:** Identify the top 100 high-volume keywords for salary pages.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
  - [**Method:**](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
    1.  [Analyze competitor salary pages (e.g., Indeed, Levels.fyi) using Screaming Frog.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
    2.  [This reveals the scale of potential URLs and keywords to target.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)

### Future Application: Augment Client

  - [The Augment client has similar 10x growth goals and also needs a larger content backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
  - [**Plan:** Document the new process for Data Annotation, then adapt it for Augment.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
  - [**Priority:** Data Annotation is the immediate focus.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Talal:**](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
      - [Record a Loom video detailing the top-down keyword research process.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
      - [Share the video with Nathalie by EOD tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
  - [**Nathalie:**](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
      - [Add the top 100 programmatic salary keywords to the Data Annotation forecast this week.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)
  - [**Liz:**](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
      - [Ensure the new process is documented and adapted for the Augment client.](https://fathom.video/share/aNZeuQBvuz7cykTvZxLqA7asGk5uDGh1?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)

action_items.description: Test top-down keyword research for Data Annotation; send initial results to Nathalie/Liz
Expand programmatic salary cluster to top 100; add to forecast w/ Talal
Analyze Indeed/Levels.fyi salary subdirectories via Screaming Frog/SEMrush; estimate scale for programmatic salary
Schedule follow-up w/ Nathalie/Liz re: keyword research; record Loom; document new strategy
